data.title: SoftBank-Backed Arm Threatens To Cancel Qualcomm's Chip Design License Ahead Of Trial In December
data.description: Qualcomm has been threatened by Arm Holdings with cancelation of its chip design license, leaving the San Diego-based semiconductor giant with 60 days notice.

data.title: Now AI Can Operate Computers Like Humans: OpenAI Rival Backed By Google Unveils Claude Upgrades Amid Fears Of Job Displacement
data.description: Anthropic said that this feature is currently in public beta on the API.

data.title: GM 'Working Hard' To Resume Driverless Cruise Vehicles By End Of Year, Says CEO Mary Barra: 'We Want To Make Sure We're Investing In Autonomy As Efficiently As Possible'
data.description: GM's autonomous driving unit, Cruise, plans to resume unsupervised testing by end of year and is working on improving technology and cost structure.

data.title: The Guy Who Tracked Elon Musk, Taylor Swift's Private Jet Wants To Know 'Who's Behind' His Suspended Social Media Accounts: '...Until Someone Threatens You Or Tells You To Do This'
data.description: Jack Sweeney, the student who gained fame for tracking private jets of billionaires and celebrities, has questioned who is behind his accounts being banned on Meta's platforms.

data.title: Gary Gensler Wishes 'Happy Sweet Sixteen' To Bitcoin Ahead Of Whitepaper Anniversary — SEC Chair Says Decentralized Tech Compatible With Securities Law, But…
data.description: SEC Chair Gary Gensler wished Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) a "Happy Sweet Sixteen" ahead of the white paper anniversary.
